question.text: For offences specified under Part B of the Schedule, they qualify as scheduled offences under PMLA only if the total value involved in such offences is:

answer.text: There is no monetary threshold for Part B offences

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Section 2(1)(y)(ii) defines scheduled offences under Part B as those where the total value involved is one crore rupees or more. This monetary threshold acts as a gateway -- customs offences under Part B must cross this value threshold to qualify as scheduled offences triggering PMLA provisions.
**The Trap:** Part A has NO monetary threshold -- all offences qualify regardless of value. Part C also has NO monetary threshold. Only Part B has the Rs. 1 crore threshold. This distinction is critical for understanding when customs-related offences trigger ML proceedings.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Series XXIV tests the Part B threshold. The IFSCA exam may present scenarios involving trade-based transactions to test whether candidates can apply the Rs. 1 crore threshold correctly.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The Rs. 1 crore threshold for Part B is important for IFSC entities in trade finance. A customs offence involving less than Rs. 1 crore would not be a scheduled offence under Part B, though it could still be examined under Part C if it has cross-border implications.
